title: alectryon hirsutus
intro: Alectryon hirsutus, also known as the hairy wood-sorrel, is a plant species in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands, and its leaves and stems are used for medicinal and culinary purposes. The plant contains flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and skin irritations. The leaves and stems of Alectryon hirsutus can be prepared as herbal infusions or teas, or used in topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects.
